title: coap_server.c: shared response buffer creates race condition
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/coap/coap_server.c
Line: 43

Global shared response buffer:
```c
static uint8_t s_response_buf[CONFIG_COAP_SERVER_MESSAGE_SIZE];
```

While the comment at line 42 says "handlers are serialized by CoAP service", this assumption may not hold:
1. Multiple CoAP services could share these handlers
2. Observer notifications (status_notify, neighbors_notify) are called from different contexts and use separate stack buffers, but share coap_resource_send path
3. If multiple interfaces have CoAP servers, responses could interleave

The safer pattern is stack-allocated buffers per handler (as done in coap_status.c).

Category: CONCURRENCY - potential data race
close_reason: Refactored to stack-allocated response buffers in all handlers and lichen_coap_respond (no global s_response_buf). Eliminates potential race. Matches coap_status.c pattern. Verified in current code (3bd50d339).
